## Accepted

## Rejected

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-6-b227564d`

finding_summary: `build_checkpoint_tree` restores ignored entries back into the temporary checkpoint index instead of dropping them, so checkpoint commits can resurrect files that were already intentionally untracked.
affected_files:
- `src/adapters/worktree.rs:265-283`
- `tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:352-438`
remediation_hint: After `git add -A` in the temp index, enumerate ignored / assume-unchanged paths in that temp index and remove them with `git update-index --force-remove -- ...`; then update the regression to assert those paths are absent from the checkpoint tree instead of preserving the parent blob.

This is not just a spec mismatch. In the `rm --cached` case covered by `worktree_adapter_preserves_parent_version_for_tracked_gitignored_paths_even_if_real_index_untracks_them`, the checkpoint commit reintroduces `ignored.txt` from the parent tree. A later `reset_to_checkpoint` will therefore re-track a file the user explicitly removed from the index, which is the opposite of the bead's goal for `result`-style paths.
